reasoning: The interim government in Bangladesh fast-tracked the approval of Starlink, with Chief Adviser Muhammad Yunus holding a call with Elon Musk in February and directing his office in early March to secure the service within three months. By March 29, the Bangladesh Investment Development Authority (BIDA) approved the service on the condition that operational activities begin within 90 days (which points to a deadline of around June 27, 2025). On April 28/29, the BTRC granted final 10-year operating licenses to the local entity, Starlink Services Bangladesh Limited. Since all regulatory hurdles are now cleared, a trial run has already been completed (April 9), and pre-orders have been accumulating for over a year, launch and activation are highly likely to occur smoothly. Starlink typically updates its map to 'Available' as soon as full orders begin shipping. The main risk is minor logistical delays (like customs handling of user terminals) pushing the launch past the tight late-June deadline and into July. However, given the strong political momentum and the 90-day maximum target from late March, a launch well before July 1 is highly probable. I estimate the chance of it launching on time at 85%.
